text: Following the completion of abdominal dissection, the surgeon positions a lone star retractor and a cylindrical trunk anoscope. A transanal purse string suture is placed at the desired height using a zero proline suture. The mucosa is marked with electrocautery outside the purse string folds, creating a circular guide. Using laparoscopic light to transilluminate the rectal wall, a full-thickness perpendicular rectotomy is performed.

The video displays a picture-in-picture view showing both the abdominal and transanal perspectives during the rectotomy phase. The laparoscopic view from the pelvic side remains stable due to the support of the bony pelvis, despite air leakage from the rectotomy. The full-layer, 360-degree rectotomy is performed, completing the rectal mobilization.

Following the completion of the rectotomy, the surgeon places an Alexis wound protector retractor, securing the internal green ring above the levator ani muscle. Under visual guidance, the bowel is gently exteriorized transanally, ensuring the mesorectum remains intact and the colon's blood supply is undamaged. The orientation of the colon and its mesentery is carefully maintained. The transaction level of the colon is determined using real-time indocyanine green imaging, after which the colon is transected.

The surgeon places an anvil in the proximal bowel and secures it with a zero-proline suture. A tubular drain is positioned on top of the anvil, and the lack of tension in the bowel is visually assessed.

The surgeon removes the wound protector and loosens the hooks of the retractor. The cylindrical anoscope is reinserted, and a purse string suture is performed at the distal rectal cuff using Xeroproline suture, ensuring full thickness stitching while avoiding inclusion of the external sphincter or the anterior vagina.

The surgeon tightens the purse string suture, automatically removing the drain from the tip. A single stapled double purse string anastomosis is created using a 31mm circular stapler under visual control. The reverse Ehrlich test is performed to assess the vascular supply of the anastomosis.
